items.prompt: Decide whether the table `(2, 6), (4, 12), (7, 21)` is proportional and justify the constant of proportionality.
items.expected_evidence: compute derivatives of bounded functions using appropriate rules and notation
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates compute derivatives of bounded functions using appropriate rules and notation with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Find the unit rate for 18 miles in 3 hours and explain what it means.
items.expected_evidence: explain why a derivative rule applies in a given bounded structural situation
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates explain why a derivative rule applies in a given bounded structural situation with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Find 15% of 80 and explain how the percent is acting as a rate per hundred.
items.expected_evidence: connect a computed derivative to slope or instantaneous rate meaning
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates connect a computed derivative to slope or instantaneous rate meaning with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Explain why equal differences alone do not prove a relationship is proportional.
items.expected_evidence: compute derivatives of bounded functions using appropriate rules and notation
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to compute derivatives of bounded functions using appropriate rules and notation.

items.prompt: Explain how a unit rate and a constant of proportionality describe the same multiplicative structure.
items.expected_evidence: explain why a derivative rule applies in a given bounded structural situation
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to explain why a derivative rule applies in a given bounded structural situation.

items.prompt: A student says a table is proportional because it increases by 3 each row. Diagnose the additive-pattern error. Focus on accumulation without rate link.
items.expected_evidence: compute derivatives of bounded functions using appropriate rules and notation
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward compute derivatives of bounded functions using appropriate rules and notation.

items.prompt: A student scales one quantity by 2 but the other by 3 and still claims proportionality. Identify the breakdown. Focus on fundamental theorem connection breakdown.
items.expected_evidence: explain why a derivative rule applies in a given bounded structural situation
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward explain why a derivative rule applies in a given bounded structural situation.

items.prompt: A map uses 1 cm for every 5 km. How far does 7.2 cm represent, and why is the relationship proportional?
items.expected_evidence: compute derivatives of bounded functions using appropriate rules and notation
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves compute derivatives of bounded functions using appropriate rules and notation in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: A recipe uses 3 cups of flour for 2 batches. Write an equation relating flour to batches and find the amount for 5 batches.
items.expected_evidence: explain why a derivative rule applies in a given bounded structural situation
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves explain why a derivative rule applies in a given bounded structural situation in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: On a delayed check, determine whether 4 notebooks for $6 and 10 notebooks for $15 are proportional and justify your reasoning.
items.expected_evidence: compute derivatives of bounded functions using appropriate rules and notation
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner still demonstrates compute derivatives of bounded functions using appropriate rules and notation on a delayed recheck without a blocking recurrence.

items.prompt: Probe limits and continuity before diagnosing derivative rules and computation, and state whether the learner is missing multiplicative structure or only the current proportional application.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether limits and continuity is secure enough to support derivative rules and computation
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in limits and continuity from a current-node issue in derivative rules and computation.
